content: Serana: You know what I love about Riften’s Ratway? It’s the perfect metaphor for Skyrim’s rotting aristocracy—filthy, decaying, and full of desperate people fucking in the shadows while the jarls pretend not to notice. Speaking of fucking, Esbern, ever consider bending over for a Draugr? Or are you too busy worshipping dead emperors? esbern: By the Nine, girl—your tongue’s as sharp as a skeever’s teeth. I’ve got more important things to dwell on than your debauched fantasies. Like prophecies of world-ending dragons, for instance. Though I suppose you’d rather debate whether Alduin prefers his victims on their knees or backs. Serana: Oh, don’t act so pious, old man. I’ve seen Blades “archives”—half those scrolls are just illustrated accounts of Akaviri orgies. And admit it, you’ve jerked off to the thought of Delphine in that tight leather armor. You’re just jealous MJR lets me deepthroat his sword while you croak about dusty dragon walls. esbern: Disgusting creature! I served emperors, not some vampire harlot’s depravity. Though I suppose you’d twist even Alduin’s return into an excuse for more deviance. “Oh, MJR, hold me tight while the world burns—it’ll be so romantic! Serana: Exactly! If Skyrim’s going to end, might as well ride it out balls-deep in pleasure. Meanwhile, you’re down here mumbling prophecies like a beggar praying for septims. Tell me, when’s the last time you felt anything besides arthritis and regret?

esbern: Enough of your filth, vampire. I've endured whispers in the dark for decades, but your prattle makes me long for the Thalmor's interrogation chambers. At least they had the decency to just carve me up quietly. Serana: Aw, did I hit a nerve? Don't worry, grandpa - when MJR and I overthrow the jarls, we'll make sure you get a nice cozy crypt. You can dust off ancient prophecies while we turn Skyrim into one giant pleasure den. Dibella would approve.
Lydia: By the sweet slick cunt of Dibella, what in Oblivion kind of robes are you wearing, old man? Looks like you raided a beggar’s closet after fucking a skeever.

Please begin / continue a conversation topic (greetings are not needed). Ensure to change the topic if the current one is losing steam. The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

Serana: So Esbern, tell me—does the ancient Blades archives ever mention if dragons fucked their victims before or after burning them alive? Asking for a friend... who might be written into history books soon. esbern: By the Eight— , forgive me—must you always drag everything into the gutter? These are sacred prophecies, not some debauched tavern tale. Serana: Oh loosen up, old man. The sacred prophecy won’t save you when the apocalypse comes and Alduin’s army starts skull-fucking the countryside. I bet even fantasize about riding a dragon bareback. esbern: Disgusting. At my age, I’m more concerned with the end times, not tallying which abominations copulate with whom. Serana: stopping the end times. Meanwhile, your precious Blades were too busy polishing their swords to even Thalmor spies infesting the Imperial Court. Priests of Talos must’ve been too distracted getting spit-roasted by their own acolytes. esbern: Your vulgarity proves why vampires were exiled from polite society. Serana: And your prudishness proves why the Empire’s losing to rebels elves. shield maneuvers with him, and I to let that amateur take first ride.
Please wrap up the current topic between the NPCs in a natural way. Nobody is leaving, so there is no need for formal goodbyes.

Serana: Well, you just keep clinging to those dusty old scrolls, Esbern. Meanwhile, I'll be over here enjoying the one prophecy that never fails—someone always ends up bent over a table by sundown. esbern: You are an obscenity upon this land. May the Divines spare me from further heresy. Serana: Spoil my fun all you want—I bet Alduin’s got better things planned for your wrinkly ass than lecturing me. Like a nice, crispy roasting. esbern: By the Nine, why must my last days be plagued by such filth...
